response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and backend software engineer with strong credentials in web technologies, TypeScript, Node.js, and low-latency systems, but the Principal Performance Architect role demands deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and deep neural network workload analysis — domains entirely absent from his profile. His low-latency work at Dunnhumby and embedded systems exposure at Altom are the closest adjacent signals, but they fall far short of the hardware-software co-design and silicon-level performance modeling expertise the role requires.
strengths: 17+ years of software engineering experience satisfies the Bachelor's + 8yr experience threshold
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby (async I/O, fibers, real-time metadata) demonstrates performance-awareness in software
Embedded systems and robotic arm work at Altom shows some breadth beyond pure web development
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or GPU workload analysis — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in AI/ML, deep learning training or inference, or AI accelerator hardware; the role is fundamentally a hardware-adjacent specialization
Educational background (two Bachelor's degrees, no Master's or PhD) and career trajectory are purely in software product engineering, not systems or hardware architecture

key_factors.factor: No deep learning or GPU architecture background
